Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

as I said on the ticket, the preferred way to fix this problem is to expand the buffer size if it is turned out small, and then call mdt_lvbo_fill() again with the new buffer. This should be handled at the end of ldlm_handle_enqueue0().

Patch Set 3:

this patch is not intended to solve the problem with mdt_lvbo_fill() problem at 100% it is solving its occurrence when max_mdsize is big enough already but is not used, exactly this causes tons of messages in log. If you want to fix also problem you mentioned it can be done in separate patch

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(3 comments)

I think the patch is correct for this particular problem, only giving -1 because it needs CDEBUG_LIMIT().

Patch Set 3:

Understood, but my point is now that we can make a completion solution with similar effort, why wouldn't we go that way?

Patch Set 3:

While effort is term of code is similar but problem will remains, we wil just fix it each time. My point is that this is just not good to introduce problem at request pack and fix it later with reply buffer grow when this can be just avoided at packing stage. For example, without that the problem happens hundred times during racer, so each that time the code in ldlm_lock_enqueue0 would resize reply buffer silently without any need. Note also that reply shrink is just memory move, but req_capsule_server_grow() includes allocation for new reply.

With patch that problem happens couple times only when mdt_max_mdsize is not yet updated.  If it is needed, that can be handled also on server as you proposed or we can let client do resend.
Uploaded patch set 4.

parents.subject: LU-10453 lnet: support gni net configuration

commit.subject: LU-10055 mdt: use max_mdsize in reply for layout intent
commit.message:
LU-10055 mdt: use max_mdsize in reply for layout intent

The LAYOUT intent reply LVB buffer size is set to a current
file layout, meanwhile it is not working when layout is changed
and the mdt_max_mdsize is better to use as size of reply buffer.
This buffer will be shrinked to the new layout size after all.

Without that change the new layout size may be bigger and layout
is not returned back, causing extra RPC from client.
The mdt_lvbo_fill() is changed also to update mdt_max_mdsize if
larger layout is found. The related message level is decreased
from D_ERROR to D_INFO.

Signed-off-by: Mikhal Pershin <mike.pershin@intel.com>
Change-Id: Iaac5dcb8b4c5aa2c050dddb5b3fb2662c59f133b
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/30004
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
